title: rosa agrestis subsp. floriana
intro: Rosa agrestis subsp. floriana, also known as Rosa floriana, is a wild species of rose found in the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa floriana include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. Rosa floriana can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive disorders, anxiety, and insomnia.
